config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena justru hak individu adalah batas agar kekuasaan tidak sewenang-wenang. Dalam pengalaman saya melihat masyarakat, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai untuk menekan suara kecil, dan itu berbahaya. Yang benar adalah mencari keseimbangan: masyarakat terlindungi, tetapi hak dasar orang tetap dihormati.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should come first in some situations, but I do not accept the idea that they should automatically outrank individual rights. From my perspective in China, people often value social stability, collective responsibility, and the common good, and that can be reasonable in public health, disaster response, or basic order. But I also think that when "society" is used too broadly, it can become an excuse to silence individuals and excuse abuse of power. The better principle is balance: protect the public interest, but keep clear limits so basic rights are not sacrificed whenever authorities claim it is for the greater good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan atas hak individu secara umum. Dalam konteks Indonesia, saya paham betul ada nilai gotong royong dan rasa kebersamaan, tetapi justru karena itu kepentingan umum sering dijadikan alasan untuk menekan yang lemah atau mengabaikan suara minoritas. Kepentingan publik memang bisa diprioritaskan dalam keadaan tertentu seperti bencana, wabah, atau keamanan, tetapi hak dasar tidak boleh diperlakukan sebagai sesuatu yang bisa dikorbankan seenaknya. Kalau batas itu hilang, “demi masyarakat” mudah berubah jadi pembenaran untuk penyalahgunaan kuasa.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the statement as a general rule, because society’s interests should not automatically outrank individual rights. From my perspective in China, I understand why people emphasize stability, order, and the common good, especially in public emergencies, but I also think that “for society” is too easily used as a blanket excuse to pressure individuals and weaken accountability. The right position is conditional priority in limited cases like disaster response or public health, not a permanent principle that lets the collective override basic rights whenever it is convenient. If we really care about society, we should protect individuals too, because a society that ignores rights eventually damages its own legitimacy.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena di Indonesia saya justru melihat “kepentingan masyarakat” sering dipakai terlalu longgar untuk membenarkan penindasan atau menutup kritik. Gotong royong dan kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi kalau hak individu bisa dikalahkan kapan saja atas nama kolektif, yang paling dirugikan biasanya kelompok kecil, minoritas, atau warga yang tidak punya kuasa. Prioritas terhadap masyarakat hanya masuk akal dalam keadaan yang sangat terbatas seperti bencana, wabah, atau ancaman keamanan nyata, dan tetap harus ada batas serta pengawasan yang ketat. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang selalu mengalahkan individu, melainkan yang bisa melindungi keduanya sekaligus.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I reject the statement as a general rule, because in my view society is not truly healthy if individual rights can be overridden whenever it is convenient. Coming from China, I understand why many people value stability, order, and collective responsibility, and I do not dismiss that at all, especially in emergencies like public health crises or disasters. But I have also seen how the language of "the greater good" can be stretched too far and become a cover for weak accountability and pressure on ordinary people. A strong society should protect the common interest without treating the individual as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
